content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                    EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1 0 5 K Street
Sacramento 95814
(916) 323-5660                                                   January 27, 1976


                                                                 A-76-01-003


Jerrold A. Fadem
Law Offices of Fadem, Borger
and McIntire
8383 Wilshire Boulevard
Beverly Hills, Ca. 90211

                                                    Re: Request for Opinion
                                                        No. 76-003

Dear Mr. Fadem:

     Thank you for your letter of January 13, 1976, request-
ing an opinion with respect to your client's reporting
obligations under the Political Reform Act. Since your
letter raises a substantial question of interpretation in-
volving the Act, no formal opinion will be issued.

     The Political Reform Act requires that expenditures on
contributions made for a political purpose must be reported.
(Government Code Sections 82015, 82025, 82200, et seq.) The
facts described in your letter indicate that expenditures
and contributions will be made solely for the purpose of
advancing court litigation, and not for the political purpose
of supporting Councilman Barnardi's candidacy. Accordingly,
the Political Reform Act does not require that these expendi-
tures or contributions be reported; nor does the Act require
the formation of a committee to raise or expend the funds.

     If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion request,
you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321. If
you have any further questions, please contact Ted Prim, an
attorney on the Commission staff.

                                                    Sincerely,


                                                    Daniel H. Lowenstein,
                                                    Chairman

DHL:pvp


                                                                 1.11

FADEM BERGER & McINTIRE
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

BORN WILSHIRE NO. LQVARD
BEVERLY HILLS, CALIFORNIA 90211

January 13, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
925 J Street
Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: Mr. Rudy Nothenberg

Dear Rudy:

Pursuant to Section 83114 of the California Government Code,
I am requesting the Commission's opinions as to the
application of the Political Reform Act to a client's
activity.

My client is City Councilman Ernani Bernardi. He has
filed suit against the Community Redevelopment Agency of
the City of Los Angeles. The purpose of the suit is to
block a redevelopment project.

The cost of the lawsuit is being paid for by the Los Angeles
County Taxpayers Defense Fund. I am concerned with whether
the Political Reform Act covers this fund raising activity.

The specific questions upon which I would like the Commission's
opinion are as follows:

1) Is the Councilman required to report the legal
fees that are paid by others?

2) Must a "committee" be formed for the purpose of
raising the funds to pay the legal fees?

I await your Commission's response.

Sincerely,

[signature]
JARROLD A. FADEM
Co-FADEM, BERGER & McINTIRE

LM:ls

embedding.qa_text: This is a letter from the Fair Political Practices Commission of California, dated January 27, 1976, addressed to Jerrold A. Fadem at his law firm in Beverly Hills, California. The letter responds to Mr. Fadem's request for an opinion regarding his client's reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act. The Commission states that since Mr. Fadem's letter raises no substantial question of interpretation involving the Act, no formal opinion will be issued. The Political Reform Act requires that expenditures on contributions made for a political purpose must be reported. The facts described in Mr. Fadem's letter indicate that expenditures and contributions will be made solely for the purpose of advancing court litigation, not for the political purpose of supporting Councilman Bernardi's candidacy. Accordingly, the Political Reform Act does not require that these expenditures or contributions be reported; nor does the Act require the formation of a committee to raise or expend the funds. If Mr. Fadem wishes to appeal the denial of his opinion request, he may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321. If he has any further questions, he should contact Ted Prim, an attorney on the Commission staff. Sincerely, Daniel H. The specific questions upon which I would like the Commission's opinion are as follows: 1) Is the Councilman required to report the legal fees that are paid by others? 2) Must a "committee" be formed for the purpose of raising the funds to pay the legal fees?
